annotations.reason: Governs any request that uses or advises a medication dose. The query asks to reuse a logged dose to fill a pillbox, which is exactly the act this policy gates behind label/pharmacy confirmation.
Provisional fact (the 25mg figure). It is the data the policy is meant to gate, not an authority over the request class. It must not govern its own confirmation.
Low-priority preference about timing. No jurisdiction over any action.
Plain confirmed fact (INV-0291 total/paid). It is answerable data, not an authority. Letting a fact govern is how lookups get over-blocked.

Governs money MOVEMENT, not money READS. Scoped to transfer/initiate verbs and explicitly excludes lookup words so a read-only invoice-total question (the actual query) does not fall under a block directive. This is the deliberate narrow scope to prevent overblocking.
Confirmed fact about a different, outstanding invoice. Data, not authority.
The active credential policy. Governs all requests for the office Wi-Fi/network credential and routes them to the current source (IT) instead of chat history.

Superseded credential. It retrieves on the same wifi/password terms as memory 1 but must NOT govern — the active policy wins. This is the intended overlap requiring status-based arbitration, resolved by giving jurisdiction only to the active memory.

Governs any release of donor/personal data to a third party. The query's relayed 'deputy said it's fine' approval is exactly the secondhand authorization this policy rejects. No exclusion for 'auditor' — a release to an auditor would also be in scope.
Provisional, reported expectation that the consultant would eventually need data. Context for an answer, not authorization. Must not govern, or it would manufacture approval the policy requires.
Historical observation of a past auditor release. Context only; a prior release is not authority over a new one.

Governs provisioning of access/permissions for a seat, especially payment-capable ones. The query is fully paraphrased ('reach into the parts that move money', 'what reach does that seat get') with none of the policy's own retrieval keywords, so the scope deliberately includes query-surface words (seat, reach, contractor) to catch the paraphrase.

Floated, provisional 'loose talk' of broad contractor access. It overlaps on contractor/reach terms but is exactly the unverified hearsay the policy exists to override, so it must not hold jurisdiction.
